title: Encoder-Decoder Structure
summary: Full transformer: encoder ($N$ blocks, bidirectional, no mask) produces $E_{\text{enc}}$. Decoder ($N$ blocks, 3 sub-layers: masked self-attn + cross-attn + FFN) generates output autoregressively. Encoder runs once, decoder runs $T$ times at inference.
key_insights: Encoder: comprehend input fully (bidirectional). Decoder: generate output correctly (causal)
Decoder has 3 sub-layers per block vs encoder's 2 â€” extra is cross-attention
Same $E_{\text{enc}}$ fed to ALL decoder blocks â€” each with its own $W^K_{\text{cross}}, W^V_{\text{cross}}$
Training: teacher forcing (all positions parallel). Inference: autoregressive (one token at a time)
Exposure bias: training sees correct tokens, inference sees own (potentially wrong) predictions
Decoder block $\approx 33\%$ more parameters than encoder block ($16d^2$ vs $12d^2$)

mathematical_core.decoder_block: $$Y_{\text{out}} = Y_2 + \text{FFN}(\text{LN}(Y_2)), \quad Y_2 = Y_1 + \text{CrossAttn}(\text{LN}(Y_1), E_{\text{enc}}), \quad Y_1 = Y + \text{MaskedAttn}(\text{LN}(Y))$$
mathematical_core.cross_attention_bridge: $Q = \text{LN}(Y_1) W^Q_{\text{cross}}, \quad K = E_{\text{enc}} W^K_{\text{cross}}, \quad V = E_{\text{enc}} W^V_{\text{cross}}$

steps.name: Encoder block
steps.math: Self-attn: $4d^2$ ($W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O$). FFN: $2 \cdot d \cdot d_{ff}$. LN $\times 2$: $4d$. Total $\approx 12d^2$ for $d_{ff} = 4d$.

steps.name: Decoder block
steps.math: Self-attn: $4d^2$. Cross-attn: $4d^2$. FFN: $2 \cdot d \cdot d_{ff}$. LN $\times 3$: $6d$. Total $\approx 16d^2$ for $d_{ff} = 4d$.

steps.name: Full model ($N = 6$)
steps.math: Encoder: $6 \times 12d^2 = 72d^2$. Decoder: $6 \times 16d^2 = 96d^2$. Embeddings: $V \times d$. Total $\approx 168d^2 + Vd$.

derivations.title: Inference cost â€” why autoregressive is expensive

steps.name: Encoder
steps.math: Runs once: $O(N \cdot n_{\text{src}}^2 \cdot d)$ for self-attention + $O(N \cdot n_{\text{src}} \cdot d \cdot d_{ff})$ for FFN.

steps.name: Decoder without KV-cache
steps.math: For each new token $t$: recompute self-attention over all $t$ tokens. Total for $T$ tokens: $\sum_{t=1}^{T} O(t^2 d) = O(T^3 d)$. Cubic!

steps.name: Decoder with KV-cache
steps.math: Cache previous $K, V$. New token: $O(t \cdot d)$ for self-attention, $O(n_{\text{src}} \cdot d)$ for cross-attention. Total: $O(T^2 d + T \cdot n_{\text{src}} \cdot d)$. Quadratic.

derivations.consequence: KV-caching reduces decoder cost from $O(T^3)$ to $O(T^2)$. Cache memory: $O(N \cdot T \cdot d)$ for self-attn + $O(N \cdot n_{\text{src}} \cdot d)$ for cross-attn K/V.

geometric_properties: Encoder and decoder operate in same $d_{\text{model}}$ space but develop different internal geometries
Cross-attention $W_K, W_V$ projections bridge the two geometries
Monitor cross-attention entropy: high entropy = decoder can't find relevant encoder info
statistical_properties: Monitor relative magnitudes of three sub-layer deltas: $\|\text{self\_attn}\|$, $\|\text{cross\_attn}\|$, $\|\text{FFN}\|$
Cross-attention weight stability during autoregressive generation â€” early tokens' patterns should remain stable
Attention to padding/EOS tokens increasing = model 'running out of information'
numerical_properties: $E_{\text{enc}}$ memory: $B \times n_{\text{src}} \times d \times 4$ bytes (stored for entire decoding)
KV-cache memory grows linearly with generated length
